title: London Borough of Enfield

infobox.value: [Joanne McCartney](/wiki/Joanne_McCartney "Joanne McCartney") AM for [Enfield and Haringey](/wiki/Enfield_and_Haringey_(London_Assembly_constituency) "Enfield and Haringey (London Assembly constituency)")

infobox.key: • [MPs](/wiki/List_of_MPs_elected_in_the_2010_United_Kingdom_general_election "List of MPs elected in the 2010 United Kingdom general election")
infobox.value: * [Feryal Clark](/wiki/Feryal_Clark "Feryal Clark") (Labour) * [Bambos Charalambous](/wiki/Bambos_Charalambous "Bambos Charalambous") (Labour) * [Kate Osamor](/wiki/Kate_Osamor "Kate Osamor") (Labour)

infobox.key: [Police](/wiki/List_of_law_enforcement_agencies_in_the_United_Kingdom "List of law enforcement agencies in the United Kingdom")

infobox.value: ([pronunciation](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/4/45/En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg/En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg "File:En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg")) is a [London borough](/wiki/London_boroughs "London boroughs") in Greater London, England. The main communities in the borough are [Edmonton](/wiki/Edmonton,_London "Edmonton, London"), [Enfield](/wiki/Enfield,_London "Enfield, London"), [Southgate](/wiki/Southgate,_London "Southgate, London") and [Palmers Green](/wiki/Palmers_Green "Palmers Green"). Enfield is an [Outer London](/wiki/Outer_London "Outer London") borough and forms part of [North London](/wiki/North_London "North London"), being the northernmost borough and bordering [Hertfordshire](/wiki/Hertfordshire "Hertfordshire") to the north and [Essex](/wiki/Essex "Essex") to the northeast. The local authority is [Enfield London Borough Council](/wiki/Enfield_London_Borough_Council "Enfield London Borough Council"), based at [Enfield Civic Centre](/wiki/Enfield_Civic_Centre "Enfield Civic Centre"). The borough's population is estimated to be 333,794.

The **London Borough of Enfield** ([pronunciation](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/4/45/En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg/En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg "File:En-uk-LBEnfield.ogg")) is a [London borough](/wiki/London_boroughs "London boroughs") in Greater London, England. The main communities in the borough are [Edmonton](/wiki/Edmonton,_London "Edmonton, London"), [Enfield](/wiki/Enfield,_London "Enfield, London"), [Southgate](/wiki/Southgate,_London "Southgate, London") and [Palmers Green](/wiki/Palmers_Green "Palmers Green"). Enfield is an [Outer London](/wiki/Outer_London "Outer London") borough and forms part of [North London](/wiki/North_London "North London"), being the northernmost borough and bordering [Hertfordshire](/wiki/Hertfordshire "Hertfordshire") to the north and [Essex](/wiki/Essex "Essex") to the northeast. The local authority is [Enfield London Borough Council](/wiki/Enfield_London_Borough_Council "Enfield London Borough Council"), based at [Enfield Civic Centre](/wiki/Enfield_Civic_Centre "Enfield Civic Centre"). The borough's population is estimated to be 333,794.

It borders the London boroughs of [Barnet](/wiki/London_Borough_of_Barnet "London Borough of Barnet") to the west, [Haringey](/wiki/London_Borough_of_Haringey "London Borough of Haringey") to the south, and [Waltham Forest](/wiki/London_Borough_of_Waltham_Forest "London Borough of Waltham Forest") to the southeast. To the north are the districts of [Hertsmere](/wiki/Hertsmere "Hertsmere"), [Welwyn Hatfield](/wiki/Welwyn_Hatfield "Welwyn Hatfield") and [Broxbourne](/wiki/Borough_of_Broxbourne "Borough of Broxbourne") (in [Hertfordshire](/wiki/Hertfordshire "Hertfordshire")), and to the east is [Epping Forest District](/wiki/Epping_Forest_District "Epping Forest District") in Essex.

## Etymology

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Etymology")]

Enfield was recorded in [Domesday Book](/wiki/Domesday_Book "Domesday Book") in 1086 as *Enefelde*, and as *Einefeld* in 1214, *Enfeld* in 1293, and *Enfild* in 1564: that is 'open land of a man called Ēana', or 'where lambs are reared', from the [Old English](/wiki/Old_English "Old English") *feld* with an Old English personal name or with Old English *ēan* 'lamb'. The *feld* would have been a reference to an area cleared of trees within woodland that would later become known as [Enfield Chase](/wiki/Enfield_Chase "Enfield Chase").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

In [Roman times](/wiki/Roman_Britain "Roman Britain"), Enfield was connected to [Londinium](/wiki/Londinium "Londinium") by [Ermine Street](/wiki/Ermine_Street "Ermine Street"), the great [Roman road](/wiki/Roman_road "Roman road") which stretched all the way up to [York](/wiki/York "York"). Artefacts found in the early 1900s reveal that there were Roman settlements in the areas that are now Edmonton and Bush Hill Park.

In 790 King [Offa of Mercia](/wiki/Offa_of_Mercia "Offa of Mercia") was recorded as giving the lands of Edmonton to [St Albans](/wiki/St_Albans "St Albans") [Abbey](/wiki/Abbey "Abbey"). The area became strategically important as [East Anglia](/wiki/East_Anglia "East Anglia") was taken over by the [Danes](/wiki/Norsemen "Norsemen"). In the 890s strongholds were built by men loyal to [King Alfred the Great](/wiki/King_Alfred_the_Great "King Alfred the Great"), in order to keep the Danes to the east of the [River Lea](/wiki/River_Lea "River Lea").

After the [Norman Conquest](/wiki/Norman_Conquest "Norman Conquest"), both Enfield and Edmonton were mentioned in [Domesday Book](/wiki/Domesday_Book "Domesday Book"). Both had churches, and Enfield had 400 inhabitants, Edmonton 300. Enfield is also described as having a "parc". This parc—a heavily forested area for hunting—was key to Enfield's existence in the [Middle Ages](/wiki/Middle_Ages "Middle Ages") (see [Enfield Old Park](/wiki/Enfield_Old_Park "Enfield Old Park")). Wealthy Londoners came to Enfield first to hunt, and then to build houses in the green, wooded surroundings. In 1303, [Edward I of England](/wiki/Edward_I_of_England "Edward I of England") granted Enfield a [charter](/wiki/Charter "Charter") to hold a weekly [market](/wiki/Market_(place) "Market (place)"), which has continued up to this day. The old market cross was removed in the early 20th century to make way for a monument to the coronation of King [Edward VII](/wiki/Edward_VII "Edward VII"), but was preserved by the horticulturalist [E. A. Bowles](/wiki/Edward_Augustus_Bowles "Edward Augustus Bowles") for his garden at nearby Myddelton House, where it remains today.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[Enfield Grammar School](/wiki/Enfield_Grammar_School "Enfield Grammar School") with its Tudor Old Hall stands next to the Enfield Town Market Place and St. Andrew's Church, the school having been extended several times since 1586. A new hall and further additions were completed shortly before World War II.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Nearby historically was the palace of [Edward VI](/wiki/Edward_VI_of_England "Edward VI of England"), where [Elizabeth I](/wiki/Elizabeth_I_of_England "Elizabeth I of England") lived while a princess, including during the final illness of [Henry VIII](/wiki/Henry_VIII_of_England "Henry VIII of England"). Edward was taken there to join her, so that in the company of his sister, [Edward Seymour, 1st Earl of Hertford](/wiki/Edward_Seymour,_1st_Earl_of_Hertford "Edward Seymour, 1st Earl of Hertford") could break the news to Edward, formally announcing the death of their royal father in the presence chamber at Enfield, on his knees to make formal obeisance to the boy as King.[[4]](#cite_note-4) Later Elizabeth held court there when she was queen (this was remembered in the name Palace Gardens that was a street running behind [Pearsons](/wiki/Pearsons "Pearsons") department store and is still recalled in the name of Enfield's shopping centre).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Industry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Industry")]

Enfield has a history of [armaments](/wiki/Armaments "Armaments") manufacture—see [Royal Small Arms Factory](/wiki/Royal_Small_Arms_Factory "Royal Small Arms Factory"). The [Lee–Enfield](/wiki/Lee%E2%80%93Enfield "Lee–Enfield") .303 rifle was standard issue for the [British Army](/wiki/British_Army "British Army") until 1957, although its usage carried on afterwards for some time. Other firearms [manufactured](/wiki/Manufactured "Manufactured") there include the [Bren](/wiki/Bren "Bren") and [Sten](/wiki/Sten "Sten") machine guns—the "en" in both cases denoting the place of manufacture.

The world's first solid state circuitry [colour televisions](/wiki/Colour_television "Colour television") were manufactured by [Ferguson](/wiki/Ferguson_Electronics "Ferguson Electronics") at their now closed plant in Enfield.

The first mass-produced [dishwasher](/wiki/Dishwasher "Dishwasher") was manufactured in [Hotpoint](/wiki/Hotpoint "Hotpoint")'s now closed Enfield plant.

The [Barclays Bank](/wiki/Barclays_Bank "Barclays Bank") branch in Enfield was the first place in the world to have an [ATM](/wiki/Automatic_Teller_Machine "Automatic Teller Machine") or cash machine; it was officially opened in June 1967 by [Reg Varney](/wiki/Reg_Varney "Reg Varney"), a television actor and personality most famous for his lead role in the comedy series *[On the Buses](/wiki/On_the_Buses "On the Buses")*. This historical event was marked by a silver plaque on the wall of the bank, and later by an [English Heritage](/wiki/English_Heritage "English Heritage") [Blue plaque](/wiki/Blue_plaque "Blue plaque").

A fine example of a [grade II listed](/wiki/Grade_II_listed "Grade II listed") [art deco](/wiki/Art_deco "Art deco") factory building can be found along Southbury Road, with the former [Ripaults Factory](/wiki/Ripaults_Factory "Ripaults Factory"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) now an office building for [Travis Perkins](/wiki/Travis_Perkins "Travis Perkins").

### Administrative history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Administrative history")]

The area of the modern borough broadly corresponds to the two [ancient parishes](/wiki/Ancient_parish "Ancient parish") of Enfield and Edmonton, and was historically part of the county of [Middlesex](/wiki/Middlesex "Middlesex"). Both parishes were made [local board districts](/wiki/Local_board_of_health "Local board of health") in 1850, each with an elected board overseeing public health and responsible for the provision of infrastructure.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The Edmonton district was divided in 1881, when its western part was made a separate district called Southgate. Such districts were reconstituted as [urban districts](/wiki/Urban_district_(England_and_Wales) "Urban district (England and Wales)") under the [Local Government Act 1894](/wiki/Local_Government_Act_1894 "Local Government Act 1894").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Each of the three urban districts was later raised to the status of a [municipal borough](/wiki/Municipal_borough "Municipal borough"): [Southgate](/wiki/Municipal_Borough_of_Southgate "Municipal Borough of Southgate") in 1933,[[9]](#cite_note-9) [Edmonton](/wiki/Municipal_Borough_of_Edmonton "Municipal Borough of Edmonton") in 1937,[[10]](#cite_note-10) and [Enfield](/wiki/Municipal_Borough_of_Enfield "Municipal Borough of Enfield") in 1955.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

The modern borough was created in 1965 under the [London Government Act 1963](/wiki/London_Government_Act_1963 "London Government Act 1963"), covering the combined area of the former boroughs of Enfield, Edmonton and Southgate, which were all abolished at the same time. The area was transferred from Middlesex to Greater London to become one of the 32 London Boroughs.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The [armorial bearings](/wiki/Armorial_bearings "Armorial bearings") of these three boroughs were also merged. The [heraldic beast](/wiki/Heraldic_beast "Heraldic beast") on the shield of the [Enfield coat of arms](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_the_London_Borough_of_Enfield "Coat of arms of the London Borough of Enfield") is known in [heraldry](/wiki/Heraldry "Heraldry") as an "[Enfield](/wiki/Enfield_(heraldry) "Enfield (heraldry)")" (or colloquially as the Enfield beast), and is used extensively as a logo representing Enfield, particularly by the borough council.

## Enfield today

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Enfield today")]

[![Church Street in Enfield Town](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fe/Church_Street_in_Enfield_Town_02.jpg/250px-Church_Street_in_Enfield_Town_02.jpg)](/wiki/File:Church_Street_in_Enfield_Town_02.jpg)

Church Street in Enfield Town

The borough's [Civic Centre](/wiki/Enfield_Civic_Centre "Enfield Civic Centre") is in Silver Street, [Enfield Town](/wiki/Enfield_Town "Enfield Town"), and is home to the council. Enfield Town is also home to the local credit union, [North London Credit Union](/wiki/North_London_Credit_Union "North London Credit Union").

In 2007, [Enfield Town](/wiki/Enfield_Town "Enfield Town") centre completed a major redevelopment project under the name PalaceXchange while retaining the Palace Gardens Shopping Centre. An extension was added to the existing retail area with many new shops, and a second [multi-storey car park](/wiki/Multi-storey_car_park "Multi-storey car park") was built along with a new road layout.

A major redevelopment of [Edmonton Green](/wiki/Edmonton,_London "Edmonton, London") including the shopping centre, and adjacent municipal housing over a wide area, started in 1999. This is still on-going, and provides new housing, health facilities, a new leisure centre, a supermarket, and many other civic features.

Many local activities are located around the [A10 road](/wiki/A10_road_(Great_Britain) "A10 road (Great Britain)"), on the sites of former industrial enterprises, which has a number of large retail outlets and a large multiplex [Cineworld](/wiki/Cineworld "Cineworld") cinema. The cinema also hosts Jubilee Church on a Sunday morning; whilst cinemagoers continue to watch films in the other screens, a [charismatic church](/wiki/Charismatic_movement "Charismatic movement") which draws its worshippers from a wide area.

The western part of Enfield is largely residential, with shopping centres in [Southgate](/wiki/Southgate,_London "Southgate, London"), [Palmers Green](/wiki/Palmers_Green "Palmers Green") and [Cockfosters](/wiki/Cockfosters "Cockfosters").

Parts of Enfield experienced [rioting in August 2011](/wiki/2011_London_riots "2011 London riots"), in which a private car and a van were set alight and completely destroyed, a police car vandalised (smashed windows) and a number of shops in Enfield Town Centre as well as others in the Enfield Retail Park being broken into and looted. The Sony Distribution Centre in the Innova Business Park, near Waltham Cross, was burnt to the ground. In September 2012, a year after the attack, a rebuilt Sony Distribution Centre was opened by the prime minister, David Cameron.[[13]](#cite_note-13)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Governance")]

Main article: [Enfield London Borough Council](/wiki/Enfield_London_Borough_Council "Enfield London Borough Council")

[![Enfield Civic Centre](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/35/Enfield_Civic_Centre_%284%29.jpg/250px-Enfield_Civic_Centre_%284%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Enfield_Civic_Centre_(4).jpg)

[Enfield Civic Centre](/wiki/Enfield_Civic_Centre "Enfield Civic Centre") seen from St Andrew's Road

The local authority is Enfield Council, based at the [Civic Centre](/wiki/Enfield_Civic_Centre "Enfield Civic Centre") on Silver Street in Enfield. The council has been controlled by a Labour Party majority since 2010.

### Greater London representation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Greater London representation")]

Since 2000, for elections to the [London Assembly](/wiki/London_Assembly "London Assembly"), the borough forms part of the [Enfield and Haringey](/wiki/Enfield_and_Haringey_(London_Assembly_constituency) "Enfield and Haringey (London Assembly constituency)") constituency.

### Members of parliament

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Members of parliament")]

The borough has three members of parliament (MPs) representing [Enfield North](/wiki/Enfield_North_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Enfield North (UK Parliament constituency)"), [Edmonton and Winchmore Hill](/wiki/Edmonton_and_Winchmore_Hill_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Edmonton and Winchmore Hill (UK Parliament constituency)") and [Southgate and Wood Green](/wiki/Southgate_and_Wood_Green_(UK_Parliament_constituency) "Southgate and Wood Green (UK Parliament constituency)") constituencies. The latter is a cross-borough constituency with neighbouring Haringey.

## Public services

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Public services")]

### Health

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Health")]

Two major [NHS](/wiki/National_Health_Service_(England) "National Health Service (England)") hospitals, [Chase Farm Hospital](/wiki/Chase_Farm_Hospital "Chase Farm Hospital") operated by the Royal Free London NHS Foundation Trust and [North Middlesex Hospital](/wiki/North_Middlesex_Hospital "North Middlesex Hospital") operated by the North Middlesex University Hospital NHS Trust, are located in the borough. NHS Enfield Clinical Commissioning Group is responsible for local primary health care, taking over this role from [Enfield Primary Care NHS Trust](/wiki/Enfield_Primary_Care_NHS_Trust "Enfield Primary Care NHS Trust") in 2013. Former hospitals in the borough include [Highlands](/wiki/Highlands_Hospital "Highlands Hospital"), which closed in 1993, and [Greentrees](/wiki/Greentrees_Hospital "Greentrees Hospital"), which closed in 1988.

### Education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Education")]

#### Schools

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Schools")]

See also: [List of schools in Enfield](/wiki/List_of_schools_in_Enfield "List of schools in Enfield")

The London Borough of Enfield is the education authority for the district. Education is provided in a mix of [community](/wiki/Community_school_(England_and_Wales) "Community school (England and Wales)"), [voluntary aided](/wiki/Voluntary_aided "Voluntary aided") and [state schools](/wiki/State_schools "State schools").[[21]](#cite_note-21) A number of private schools are also located in the borough.

The borough is home to the well-renowned [Enfield Grammar School](/wiki/Enfield_Grammar_School "Enfield Grammar School"), founded in 1558, which still uses its [Tudor](/wiki/Tudor_dynasty "Tudor dynasty") building which is now often referred to as the Old Hall. Enfield Grammar School is based in the centre of Enfield Town. [St Ignatius' College](/wiki/St_Ignatius%27_College "St Ignatius' College") is the borough's largest Catholic school and one of the best-performing non-selective schools in the borough, at both [GCSE](/wiki/General_Certificate_of_Secondary_Education "General Certificate of Secondary Education") and [A-Level](/wiki/Advanced_Level_(UK) "Advanced Level (UK)").[[22]](#cite_note-results-22) [Bishop Stopford's School](/wiki/The_Bishop_Stopford%27s_School "The Bishop Stopford's School") is Enfield's largest school, and [The College of Haringey, Enfield and North East London](/wiki/The_College_of_Haringey,_Enfield_and_North_East_London "The College of Haringey, Enfield and North East London") also has a campus in the borough.

In 2007 a new school named [Oasis Academy Enfield](/wiki/Oasis_Academy_Enfield "Oasis Academy Enfield") was opened, following the takeover of Albany by the Oasis Company as [Oasis Academy Hadley](/wiki/Oasis_Academy_Hadley "Oasis Academy Hadley").
[The Latymer School](/wiki/The_Latymer_School,_Edmonton "The Latymer School, Edmonton") is another Grammar school in the borough, and is based in the Edmonton area.

Enfield County School is an all girls comprehensive school which sits opposite Enfield Grammar Boys school in Enfield town.

#### University

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: University")]

[Middlesex University](/wiki/Middlesex_University "Middlesex University"), the former polytechnic, used to have two campuses in Enfield: Cat Hill and [Trent Park](/wiki/Trent_Park "Trent Park"). These sites both closed in 2011 and courses were relocated to the Hendon campus.

[Oak Hill Theological College](/wiki/Oak_Hill_Theological_College "Oak Hill Theological College"), an associate college of Middlesex University, is located in the borough.

[Capel Manor](/wiki/Capel_Manor "Capel Manor") College also offers various courses.

### London Fire Brigade

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: London Fire Brigade")]

The London Borough of Enfield has three fire stations; Edmonton, Enfield and Southgate. However, if an incident requires a higher attendance, appliances from throughout London can be mobilised to meet the needs of the area if required.[[23]](#cite_note-23)

The three fire stations operate a total of five pumping appliances, one rescue tender, a command unit and a USAR unit.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

Of the three fire stations within the borough, Enfield is mobilised to the most incidents and has the largest station ground (the area of which Enfield is the first appliance to attend).[[25]](#cite_note-25)

### Museums

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Museums")]

The borough's museums include [Forty Hall](/wiki/Forty_Hall "Forty Hall") and the garden exhibitions at [Capel Manor](/wiki/Forty_Hill "Forty Hill"), [Myddleton House](/wiki/Forty_Hill "Forty Hill") (part of the [Lee Valley Park](/wiki/Lee_Valley_Park "Lee Valley Park")) and the Whitewebbs Museum of Transport.

The Borough's own museum is based in Thomas Hardy House, 39 London Road.

## Sport and recreation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Sport and recreation")]

[![QE2 Stadium, home to the country's first fan-owned football club, Enfield Town FC](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/81/QE2_Stadium_05.jpg/250px-QE2_Stadium_05.jpg)](/wiki/File:QE2_Stadium_05.jpg)

QE2 Stadium, home to the country's first fan-owned football club, Enfield Town FC

Enfield's [King George's Field](/wiki/List_of_King_George_V_Playing_Fields_(Greater_London) "List of King George V Playing Fields (Greater London)"), named in memory of [King George V](/wiki/George_V_of_the_United_Kingdom "George V of the United Kingdom"), includes the [Queen Elizabeth II](/wiki/Elizabeth_II_of_the_United_Kingdom "Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom") athletics stadium, the Enfield Ignatians Rugby Club and numerous [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football"), [rugby](/wiki/Rugby_football "Rugby football") and baseball diamonds. The playing fields were used as a [POW](/wiki/Prisoner_of_War "Prisoner of War") camp for Italians during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").

The second largest playing fields are at Firs Farm on Firs Lane. There are a handful of rugby pitches along with more than a dozen football pitches. These are used by local amateur football clubs including [Winchmore Hill Football Club](/wiki/Winchmore_Hill_Football_Club "Winchmore Hill Football Club").[[27]](#cite_note-winchmorehillfc.co.uk-27)

Many sports teams and clubs are located in Enfield; Winchmore Hill Sports Club, [Winchmore Hill Football Club](/wiki/Winchmore_Hill_Football_Club "Winchmore Hill Football Club"),[[27]](#cite_note-winchmorehillfc.co.uk-27) [Enfield Town F.C.](/wiki/Enfield_Town_F.C. "Enfield Town F.C."), [Enfield 1893 F.C.](/wiki/Enfield_1893_F.C. "Enfield 1893 F.C."), [Cockfosters F.C.](/wiki/Cockfosters_F.C. "Cockfosters F.C."), [Enfield Borough F.C.](/wiki/Enfield_Borough_F.C. "Enfield Borough F.C.") and Futsal Club Enfield are the most prominent. The once successful football club, Enfield FC, were forced into a nomadic existence after the sale of their Southbury Road ground and became defunct at the end of the 2006/2007 season after years of ground sharing at many Hertfordshire based clubs. Enfield 1893 were formed after Enfield FC's demise and have spent time groundsharing at [Ware FC](/wiki/Ware_FC "Ware FC") and Broxbourne Borough FC. They finally found a ground in the borough when they merged with [Brimsdown Rovers](/wiki/Brimsdown_Rovers "Brimsdown Rovers") for the start of the 2011/2012 season.

The principal cricket clubs in the area are Enfield,[[28]](#cite_note-www.enfieldcricketclub.com-28) [Winchmore Hill Cricket Club](/wiki/Winchmore_Hill_Cricket_Club "Winchmore Hill Cricket Club"),[[29]](#cite_note-winchmorehillcc.co.uk-29) Edmonton and Southgate, with many others playing cricket in and around the borough such as, Enfield Invicta, Mayfield CC, Myddleton House, [North Enfield CC](/wiki/North_Enfield_CC "North Enfield CC"), Botany Bay and Holtwhites Trinibis CC. Many clubs play in either the Middlesex Cricket League or the Hertfordshire League. Enfield, Southgate, and Winchmore Hill have long been the area's most successful cricket clubs, regularly competing in the Middlesex Premier League. Enfield (in 1988) and Southgate (in 1977) are both past winners of the [ECB National Club Cricket Championship](/wiki/ECB_National_Club_Cricket_Championship "ECB National Club Cricket Championship").

Gaelic Football is also played in the area.

Enfield Phoenix is the local basketball club with teams playing in the National League and the Central London League.

Theatre goers are catered for by the [Intimate Theatre](/wiki/Intimate_Theatre "Intimate Theatre"), the [Millfield Theatre](/wiki/Millfield_Theatre "Millfield Theatre"), the Dugdale Centre[[30]](#cite_note-30)[[31]](#cite_note-31) and the [Chickenshed Theatre Company](/wiki/Chickenshed_Theatre_Company "Chickenshed Theatre Company"), with amateur and professional shows, dance pieces, musicals and live music on show. Amateur dramatic companies include Saint Monica's Players, The Capel Players and The London Pantomimers.[[32]](#cite_note-32) Talkies Community Cinema screens British Independent and specialist films in a range of venues across the borough.

[Tottenham Hotspur Football Club](/wiki/Tottenham_Hotspur_Football_Club "Tottenham Hotspur Football Club")'s new training ground is located in Enfield at [Bulls Cross](/wiki/Bulls_Cross "Bulls Cross").[[33]](#cite_note-33)

Enfield is the home to the annual Livestock Music Festival.[[34]](#cite_note-34)

[Croquet](/wiki/Croquet "Croquet") is played at [Enfield Croquet Club](/wiki/Enfield_Croquet_Club "Enfield Croquet Club") in Bush Hill Park.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

The first international branch of the [WWE Performance Center](/wiki/WWE_Performance_Center "WWE Performance Center") was opened in Enfield's Great Cambridge Industrial Estate on 11 January 2019. The center is used by the [WWE](/wiki/WWE "WWE") as a [professional wrestling school](/wiki/Glossary_of_professional_wrestling_terms#S "Glossary of professional wrestling terms") to train potential future wrestlers from across the globe, as well as a [training](/wiki/Practice_(learning_method) "Practice (learning method)") centre for current WWE wrestlers.[[36]](#cite_note-36)

## Transport

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=London_Borough_of_Enfield&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: Transport")]

In 1840 the first section of the [Northern and Eastern Railway](/wiki/Northern_and_Eastern_Railway "Northern and Eastern Railway") was opened from [Stratford](/wiki/Stratford,_London "Stratford, London") to [Broxbourne](/wiki/Broxbourne "Broxbourne"), with stations at Water Lane ([Angel Road](/wiki/Angel_Road_railway_station "Angel Road railway station")) and [Ponders End](/wiki/Ponders_End_railway_station "Ponders End railway station"). Further stations were added in 1855 at [Enfield Lock](/wiki/Enfield_Lock_railway_station "Enfield Lock railway station") ([Royal Small Arms Factory](/wiki/Royal_Small_Arms_Factory "Royal Small Arms Factory")) and 1884 at [Brimsdown](/wiki/Brimsdown_railway_station "Brimsdown railway station"). A branch line from Water Lane to [Enfield Town](/wiki/Enfield_Town_railway_station "Enfield Town railway station") was opened in 1849 serving [Edmonton](/wiki/Edmonton,_London "Edmonton, London") Low Level and Enfield Town. A further station was added in 1880 at [Bush Hill Park](/wiki/Bush_Hill_Park_railway_station "Bush Hill Park railway station"). The [direct line](/wiki/Stoke_Newington_%26_Edmonton_Railway "Stoke Newington & Edmonton Railway") from London to Enfield Town was opened in 1872 with stations at Silver Street and Lower Edmonton (now called Edmonton Green). In 1891, a loop from Edmonton serving [Southbury](/wiki/Southbury_railway_station "Southbury railway station") (Churchbury) and [Turkey Street](/wiki/Turkey_Street_railway_station "Turkey Street railway station") (Forty Hill) to [Cheshunt](/wiki/Cheshunt_railway_station "Cheshunt railway station") on the main line was added. The Cheshunt and Enfield Town services are currently served by [London Overground](/wiki/London_Overground "London Overground").

In 1871 the [Great Northern Railway](/wiki/Great_Northern_Railway_(Great_Britain) "Great Northern Railway (Great Britain)") opened [its station](/wiki/Enfield_Chase_railway_station "Enfield Chase railway station") on Windmill Hill. This was later replaced in 1910 when the line was extended to [Cuffley](/wiki/Cuffley_railway_station "Cuffley railway station"). This section of railway is now part of the line commonly known as the [Hertford Loop](/wiki/Hertford_Loop_Line "Hertford Loop Line").

[![Arnos Grove Station on the Piccadilly Line](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2e/Arnos_Grove_Station_%2825%29.jpg/250px-Arnos_Grove_Station_%2825%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Arnos_Grove_Station_(25).jpg)

Arnos Grove station on the Piccadilly line

Enfield is served by the [Piccadilly line](/wiki/Piccadilly_line "Piccadilly line") of the [London Underground](/wiki/London_Underground "London Underground") with stations at [Arnos Grove](/wiki/Arnos_Grove_tube_station "Arnos Grove tube station"), [Southgate](/wiki/Southgate_tube_station "Southgate tube station"), [Oakwood](/wiki/Oakwood_tube_station "Oakwood tube station") (named as Enfield West when it first opened) and [Cockfosters](/wiki/Cockfosters_tube_station "Cockfosters tube station") (where the line terminates). Suburban [London Overground](/wiki/London_Overground "London Overground") and [National Rail](/wiki/National_Rail "National Rail") [Great Northern](/wiki/Thameslink_and_Great_Northern "Thameslink and Great Northern") services also run within the borough.

There are numerous bus services throughout the borough run by [Transport for London](/wiki/Transport_for_London "Transport for London").

The main roads running through the borough include the [North Circular Road](/wiki/A406_road "A406 road"), the [M25](/wiki/M25_motorway "M25 motorway"), [Green Lanes](/wiki/Green_Lanes_(London) "Green Lanes (London)"), the [A10](/wiki/A10_road_(Great_Britain) "A10 road (Great Britain)") and the old [Hertford Road](/wiki/Hertford_Road "Hertford Road") (A1010).

In March 2011, the main forms of transport that residents used to travel to work were: driving a car or van, 26.1% of all residents aged 16–74; underground, metro, light rail, tram, 8.2%; bus, minibus or coach, 8.2%; train, 7.3%; on foot, 4.0%; work mainly at or from home, 2.7%; passenger in a car or van, 1.6%.[[37]](#cite_note-37)
